title: Overnight Shelter & Basic Services
description: Overnight emergency shelter for adult men and women at Cityteam's Grand Ave location, open 7 nights a week from 7pm to 7am. A recovery-oriented site — guests are expected to arrive sober. Provides a safe place to sleep, a hot meal, showers, and clean clothing.
engage: Come to 526 SE Grand Ave, Portland, in the evening (doors open around 7pm), or call 503-231-9334 / email portlandinfo@cityteam.org for details.
content:
Services

  • Safe overnight shelter (7pm–7am, 7 days a week)
  • Hot cooked meals
  • Showers
  • Clean clothing
  • Connection to Cityteam's recovery programs

Who It's For

Adult men and women experiencing homelessness. This is a recovery-oriented shelter, so guests are expected to arrive sober.

How to Access

Walk in during evening hours at 526 SE Grand Ave. Call 503-231-9334 with questions.

title: Renew Recovery Program
description: Cityteam's faith-based residential recovery program for men working to overcome substance use — offering structure, shelter, meals, counseling, and a pathway back to stability and community.
engage: Call 503-231-9334 or email portlandinfo@cityteam.org to ask about entering the Renew recovery program.
content:
Program

Renew is Cityteam's recovery program for men struggling with addiction, providing a structured, supportive residential environment with meals, shelter, counseling, and spiritual support on the path to recovery and reintegration.

Who It's For

Men seeking to overcome substance use and rebuild their lives.

How to Access

Contact Cityteam Portland at 503-231-9334 or portlandinfo@cityteam.org.

title: Community of Hope (Women & Children)
description: Cityteam's program serving women and children experiencing homelessness, offering shelter and family-centered support. Community of Hope became part of Cityteam Portland in January 2024.
engage: Call 503-231-9334 or email portlandinfo@cityteam.org to learn about Community of Hope services for women and children.
content:
Program

Community of Hope provides shelter and support for women and children experiencing homelessness. It joined Cityteam Portland on January 1, 2024, extending Cityteam's services to families.

Who It's For

Women and children experiencing homelessness.

How to Access

Contact Cityteam Portland at 503-231-9334 or portlandinfo@cityteam.org.

title: Night Strike (Thursday Night Outreach)
description: A Thursday-night street outreach under the Burnside Bridge in Portland, 7:30–9:30pm. People experiencing homelessness can get a hot meal, replacement clothing, shoes and sleeping bags, hygiene supplies, and free haircuts — plus connection and community. Volunteers welcome too.
engage: Come to the Burnside Bridge on Thursday evenings 7:30–9:30pm. To volunteer, visit cityteam.org/get-involved/nightstrike or call 503-231-9334.
content:
What's Offered

  • Hot meal
  • Replacement clothing, shoes, and sleeping bags
  • Personal hygiene and grooming supplies
  • Free haircuts and shaves
  • Foot washing
  • Connection and community

When & Where

Thursday evenings, 7:30–9:30pm, under the Burnside Bridge, Portland.

Get Involved

Night Strike is also a volunteer opportunity — volunteers help serve meals, distribute supplies, and connect with houseless neighbors.

title: Court Record Relief
description: Free legal help clearing criminal records for low-income Oregonians — expungements, SB 819 applications for relief on otherwise non-expungeable felonies, and motions to waive court fines and fees.
engage: Complete the intake form at clear-clinic.org/record-relief, or visit a drop-in clinic (no appointment needed). Call 503-389-5919.
content:
Services

  • Criminal record expungements
  • SB 819 applications for relief on non-expungeable felonies
  • Motions to waive court fines and fees

Who It's For

Low-income Oregonians with criminal records.

How to Access

Submit the online intake form or attend a drop-in clinic (Portland metro, Oregon prisons, tribal nations, and across Oregon — no appointment needed).

title: Immigration Justice
description: Free immigration legal services — deportation defense, DACA renewals, work permits, and support for immigrant workers facing mistreatment.
engage: Use the DACA or enrollment intake forms at clear-clinic.org/immigration-justice, or call 503-389-5919.
content:
Services

  • Deportation / removal defense
  • DACA renewals
  • Work permit applications
  • Immigrant worker justice

Who It's For

Immigrants facing deportation or workplace mistreatment.

How to Access

Complete the relevant intake form online, attend a drop-in clinic, or call 503-389-5919.

title: Name & Gender-Marker Changes
description: Free legal help with legal name changes and gender-marker changes, including advice and guidance throughout the process.
engage: Complete the intake form at clear-clinic.org/name-gendermarker-changes, or call 503-389-5919.
content:
Services

  • Legal name changes
  • Gender-marker changes
  • Legal advice throughout the process

Who It's For

Anyone in Oregon seeking to change their legal name or gender marker.

How to Access

Submit the online intake form or attend a drop-in clinic.

title: Housing Justice
description: Free tenant legal help — eviction defense, eviction record expungement, and tenant advocacy, including a tenant hotline. Eviction defense is focused on Portland; other help is statewide.
engage: Call the tenant hotline at 503-433-6431 (Tue–Thu, 9am–5pm), submit the advocacy or eviction-expungement intake form, or call 503-389-5919.
content:
Services

  • Eviction defense (Portland)
  • Eviction record expungement
  • Tenant advocacy and rights
  • Tenant hotline: 503-433-6431 (Tue–Thu, 9am–5pm)

Who It's For

Low-income tenants facing eviction (defense focused on Portland; expungement and advocacy statewide).

How to Access

Call the hotline, submit an intake form, or attend a drop-in clinic.

title: Withdrawal Management (Detox)
description: Medically-supervised withdrawal management (detox) at Coda's Beaverton Recovery Center — a safe, supported start to recovery from alcohol and other drugs.
engage: Call 855-733-2632 (855-SEE-CODA) or complete the patient intake form at codainc.org to get started.
content:
Program

Medically-supervised detoxification providing 24-hour support as your body withdraws from substances, in a secure and structured setting. A first step that transitions into further treatment.

Location

Beaverton Recovery Center.

How to Access

Call 855-SEE-CODA (855-733-2632) or fill out the patient intake form online. A waitlist may apply until beds are available.

title: Residential Treatment
description: Inpatient, gender-specific residential addiction treatment averaging 90 days, with 24-hour support, individual and group counseling, medication-assisted treatment, case management, and family involvement. Specialized care for pregnant and parenting women.
engage: Call 855-733-2632 (855-SEE-CODA) or complete the patient intake form at codainc.org.
content:
Program

Live-in treatment in a secure, structured therapeutic environment. Average length of stay is 90 days.

What's Included

  • Individual and group counseling
  • Medication-assisted treatment
  • 24-hour support
  • Case management and family involvement
  • Peer support and 12-step connections
  • Transition to other levels of care

Locations

  • Women: Beaverton Recovery Center; pregnant and parenting women at Gresham Recovery Center
  • Men: Gresham, Tigard, or Beaverton Recovery Centers

How to Access

Call 855-SEE-CODA (855-733-2632) or use the online intake form. A waitlist may apply.

title: Opioid Treatment Program
description: Coda runs Oregon's oldest opioid addiction treatment program, offering medication-assisted treatment (methadone/buprenorphine) with counseling and support at multiple recovery centers.
engage: Call 855-733-2632 (855-SEE-CODA) or complete the patient intake form at codainc.org.
content:
Program

Specialized opioid use disorder treatment combining medication (such as methadone or buprenorphine) with counseling and wraparound support. CODA operates the oldest opioid treatment program in Oregon.

Locations

Beaverton Recovery Center, Portland Recovery Center, and Seaside Recovery Center.

How to Access

Call 855-SEE-CODA (855-733-2632) or fill out the patient intake form online.

title: Outpatient Treatment
description: Non-residential addiction counseling and therapy at Coda's outpatient centers, letting you get treatment while living at home and keeping work, school, and family commitments. On-site and virtual options.
engage: Call 855-733-2632 (855-SEE-CODA) or complete the patient intake form at codainc.org.
content:
Program

Outpatient counseling and therapy for substance use, with on-site and remote virtual care options.

Locations

Clackamas, Hillsboro, and Tigard Outpatient Centers.

How to Access

Call 855-SEE-CODA (855-733-2632) or use the online intake form.

title: Supported Housing
description: Recovery-focused supported housing at Stepping Stones in Portland, giving people in recovery a stable, structured place to live while continuing treatment and rebuilding.
engage: Call 855-733-2632 (855-SEE-CODA) or ask Coda admissions about Stepping Stones supported housing.
content:
Program

Recovery-focused residential support providing stable, structured housing for people in treatment and early recovery.

Location

Stepping Stones, Portland.

How to Access

Call 855-SEE-CODA (855-733-2632) or coordinate with Coda admissions.

title: Furniture Bank
description: A furniture bank providing gently-used furniture and household goods — beds, couches, dining sets, dressers, and housewares — to people moving into stable housing. Access is through a case manager or advocate at a partner agency. A flat service fee applies; delivery available for a fee.
engage: Work with your case manager or advocate to schedule an appointment. No case manager? Call 211 or contact a Community Warehouse partner agency. Locations in Portland, Tigard, and Gresham.
content:
What's Provided

Gently-used furniture and household items — mattresses, couches, dining furniture, dressers, and housewares — to help furnish a home.

Who It's For

People moving into stable housing, including those exiting homelessness, refugees and immigrants, veterans, people leaving incarceration, domestic violence survivors, and members of BIPOC and LGBTQIA+ communities.

How to Access

  • You must work with a case manager or advocate from a Community Warehouse partner agency.
  • No case manager? Call 211 or contact a partner organization.
  • Your case manager schedules a one-hour appointment (Portland, Tigard, or Gresham) to select items — in person or virtual — and must attend.

Cost

A flat service fee applies (your case manager can determine if their agency covers it). Delivery available for additional fees.
street_address: 3961 NE Martin Luther King Jr Blvd

title: Supported Living
description: In-home staff support that helps people with disabilities live independently in their own homes — daily living assistance tailored to each person's goals and needs.
engage: Call 503-292-4964 or email info@cvision.org to learn about Supported Living services.
content:
Program

Community Vision provides in-home staff assistance so people with disabilities can direct their own lives and live independently at home, with support matched to their individual needs.

Who It's For

People with disabilities living in their own homes.

How to Access

Contact Community Vision at 503-292-4964 or info@cvision.org.

title: Housing Access
description: Guidance for people with disabilities on finding and keeping an apartment — navigating the search, applications, and staying housed.
engage: Call 503-292-4964 or email info@cvision.org about the Housing Access program.
content:
Program

Support for people with disabilities in locating and maintaining housing — help with the apartment search, applications, and keeping a home over time.

Who It's For

People with disabilities seeking housing.

How to Access

Contact Community Vision at 503-292-4964 or info@cvision.org.

title: Employment Connections
description: Employment support for job seekers with disabilities — job search assistance, on-site training, and ongoing workplace support.
engage: Call 503-292-4964 or email info@cvision.org about Employment Connections.
content:
Program

Help finding and keeping a job, including job search assistance, on-the-job training, and continued workplace support.

Who It's For

Job seekers with disabilities.

How to Access

Contact Community Vision at 503-292-4964 or info@cvision.org.

title: Assistive Technology Lab
description: Consultations and resources to help people with disabilities find technology solutions that support independence, plus professional trainings for support staff.
engage: Visit cv-atlab.org or call 503-292-4964 to book an assistive technology consultation.
content:
Program

The Assistive Technology Lab offers consultations and resources for finding technology solutions that increase independence for people with disabilities, and provides professional trainings for support professionals.

Who It's For

People with disabilities and the professionals who support them.

How to Access

Visit cv-atlab.org or contact Community Vision at 503-292-4964.
